decision_point: In an older adult (age >=75) with a displaced isolated olecranon fracture, is nonoperative management or surgical fixation preferred?
clinical_vignette:
Typical adult patient for whom this decision arises (no atypical comorbidities or contraindications).

Decision: In an older adult (age >=75) with a displaced isolated olecranon fracture, is nonoperative management or surgical fixation preferred?
Options:
  1. Nonoperative management
  2. Surgical fixation
label: patient_dependent
label_rationale: Randomized data show comparable DASH scores at 1 year; surgical groups have extremely high complication rates (up to 80%), and nonunions in the nonoperative group are mostly asymptomatic and well-tolerated.

reviews.notes: stratum via llm_haiku — This represents guideline discordance and practice variation in geriatric olecranon fracture management, with no famous landmark trial or eponymous technique driving the debate, despite emerging evidence favoring nonoperative management in older adults.
